composition.Programmer 1:
Fix and complete the main backend Python file 'main.py' for the WeatherForecast web application. This includes 
- Ensuring Flask app is properly configured to locate the 'templates/' directory so that render_template calls for all eight pages (Dashboard, Current Weather, Weekly Forecast, Location Search, Weather Alerts, Air Quality, Saved Locations, and Settings) work without TemplateNotFound errors.
- Verify and fix any path or configuration issues related to template loading.
- Maintain all existing backend logic for reading/writing local text files in 'data/' directory, processing user inputs, and routing.
- Coordinate with Programmer 2 to ensure backend routes match frontend template filenames and locations.
composition.Programmer 2:
Implement and organize all frontend HTML templates in the 'templates/' directory for the WeatherForecast web application. This includes 
- Creating the eight HTML template files  dashboard.html, current_weather.html, weekly_forecast.html, location_search.html, weather_alerts.html, air_quality.html, saved_locations.html, and settings.html.
- Ensure that each template strictly follows the detailed page design and element IDs specified in the original task.
- Use Flask's url_for for all internal links and static assets.
- Coordinate with Programmer 1 to ensure template filenames and element IDs match backend expectations and routing.
